subject: Geography
title: Sacred Caves
description: A short lesson to help you apply Sacred Caves.

hook.text: The Cave of the Patriarchs in Hebron is sacred to Jews, Christians, and Muslims alike.

buildup.text: Across cultures, caves represent the underworld, rebirth, and contact with the divine.

discovery.text: Buddhist monks carved 1,500 temples into the cliffs at Ajanta, India, over 800 years.

twist.text: The Dead Sea Scrolls—the oldest biblical manuscripts—were found hidden in a cave by a shepherd boy.

climax.text: Caves have been temples, tombs, and vaults for humanity's most sacred treasures.

punchline.text: What we hold most sacred, we hide in the earth.

quiz.question: Where were the Dead Sea Scrolls found?
options: Hidden in a cave near the Dead Sea
Buried under a temple in Jerusalem
